stemHtml: A modern jet transport aeroplane is cruising at 35000 ft in an area of mountain waves. The cruising speed is at M 0.79 with M<sub>MO</sub> at M 0.81 when the aeroplane suddenly experiences a positive wind shear. The autothrust is slow at reacting and the aeroplane reaches a speed of M 0.82 before starting to decelerate again. Apart from an overspeed warning, are there any other indications of the overspeed that may be apparent to the pilots?

options.a: with the feet on the ground, a slight vibration is felt through the fuselage structure, otherwise the aeroplane observes normal behaviour.
options.b: the aeroplane is buffeting and a significant pitch-down moment suddenly develops, requiring an immediate corrective action to avoid entering a dive.
options.c: strong shock waves will be forming, creating separation of the boundary layer, causing a significant buffet as the aeroplane enters a high-speed shock stall.
options.d: unusually high levels of ambient wind noise followed by severe buffeting requiring a pitch-up input to ensure a rapid deceleration to a speed below M<sub>MO</sub>.
